title: Black-Scholes Model: What is a key characteristic of the Black-Scholes model in...
summary: Evaluate the Black-Scholes model's assumptions and limitations for option pricing.
prompt: What is a key characteristic of the Black-Scholes model in option pricing?

options.text: A key characteristic of the Black-Scholes model is that it assumes constant

solution: A key characteristic of the Black-Scholes model is that it assumes constant volatility over time, which simplifies the pricing of options. This assumption allows the model to calculate the theoretical value of call and put options based on the underlying asset's price, strike price, time to expiration, risk-free rate, and volatility. However, this assumption may not hold true in real markets, where volatility can change frequently due to varying market conditions.
